subject: Political Science
overall_learning_objectives: Understand the meaning and concept of federalism
Identify the key features of a federal system
Understand how federalism works in India
Learn about the distribution of powers between Union and State governments
Understand the formation of linguistic states
Understand decentralisation and the role of local governments

lesson_plan.title: Introduction to Federalism
student_activities: Identify federal countries on a world map
Discuss why large countries adopt federal systems

lesson_plan.title: Key Features of Federalism
topics_covered: Two or more levels of government
teaching_strategy: Use of diagrams showing levels of government
student_activities: Draw a diagram showing levels of government

lesson_plan.title: Types of Federations
teaching_strategy: Use of examples like USA and India
student_activities: Create a comparison chart of two types of federations

lesson_plan.title: Distribution of Powers in India
teaching_strategy: Board demonstration of the three lists
student_activities: Categorize subjects into Union, State and Concurrent lists
Discuss why power distribution is necessary

lesson_plan.title: Linguistic States and Language Policy
student_activities: Identify states formed on linguistic basis
List languages spoken in their region

lesson_plan.title: Centre–State Relations
student_activities: Discuss examples of cooperation between centre and states
Write short notes on coalition governments

lesson_plan.title: Decentralisation and Local Government
student_activities: Draw the structure of Panchayati Raj

teaching_aids: World map and India political map
teacher_tip: Use maps, diagrams, and real-life political examples to help students understand how federalism functions practically in India.
